                                           'id': '63637417847d336a2db377f9',

                           'content': '<p>In the fast-moving world of '
                                      'artificial intelligence, a week is a '
                                      'long time. For OpenClaw (formerly '
                                      'Clawdbot and then MoltBot), the last '
                                      'seven days have been a masterclass in '
                                      'viral growth, but trademark disputes '
                                      'and a stream of mishaps have marred its '
                                      'potential.</p><p>What started as a '
                                      'niche tool for developers has turned '
                                      'into a cautionary tale of "The Great '
                                      'Molt" — a rebrand that saw millions of '
                                      'dollars vanish in minutes while '
                                      'simultaneously cementing OpenClaw as a '
                                      'potential game-changer for crypto '
                                      'degens.</p><h2 '
                                      'id="what-is-openclaw">What is '
                                      'OpenClaw?</h2><p>OpenClaw is a '
                                      'self-hosted <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/ai-agents" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow">AI '
                                      'agent</a> assistant created by '
                                      'developer Peter Steinberger. Unlike a '
                                      'standard chatbot, OpenClaw is designed '
                                      'to live on your local hardware — often '
                                      'a Mac Mini — and execute real-world '
                                      'tasks.</p><p>It can:</p><ul><li>Control '
                                      'your browser</li><li>Run terminal '
                                      'commands</li><li>Plan and execute tasks '
                                      'autonomously</li><li>Communicate with '
                                      'you via WhatsApp or '
                                      'Telegram.</li></ul><p>Its "local-first" '
                                      'architecture means your private data '
                                      'never leaves your server, a feature '
                                      'that has made it popular among '
                                      'privacy-conscious crypto '
                                      'natives.</p><p>It launched publicly in '
                                      'November 2025, but didn’t gain '
                                      'widespread attention until December '
                                      '2025 following the success of '
                                      'Anthropic’s Claude Code.</p><p>OpenClaw '
                                      'has since gone viral, <a '
                                      'href="https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">recording</a> over 100,000 '
                                      'stars on <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/github" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">GitHub</a> (similar to '
                                      'Instagram likes) and securing mentions '
                                      'in high-profile publications including '
                                      '<a '
                                      'href="https://www.forbes.com/sites/ronschmelzer/2026/01/27/viral-ai-sidekick-clawdbot-changes-name-to-moltbot-and-sheds-its-old-skin/" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">Forbes</a> and <a '
                                      'href="https://www.wired.com/story/clawdbot-moltbot-viral-ai-assistant/" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">WIRED</a> — making it one of '
                                      'the most visible <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/open-source" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">open-source</a> agent tools '
                                      'right now.</p><h2 id="the-molt">The '
                                      '“Molt”</h2><p>OpenClaw’s rise to glory '
                                      'wasn’t without hiccups. The project was '
                                      'originally named Clawdbot, but it '
                                      'received a trademark request from AI '
                                      'development firm Anthropic due to its '
                                      'similarity to their "Claude" large '
                                      'language model.</p><p>The team pivoted '
                                      'to the name MoltBot — a metaphor for a '
                                      'lobster shedding its shell to '
                                      'grow.</p><p>But the transition became a '
                                      'disaster when the team attempted to '
                                      'swap their social media '
                                      'handles.</p><p>In the few seconds '
                                      'between dropping the old @clawdbot '
                                      'handle and claiming the new one, '
                                      'professional "handle snipers" snagged '
                                      'it.</p><img '
                                      'src="https://academy-public.coinmarketcap.com/optimized-uploads/1d2f30a6b2f04bd28dc56593bdde5e02.jpg"><p><em>Source: '
                                      '<a '
                                      'href="https://twitter.com/steipete/status/2016068265391354181" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">Peter '
                                      'Steinberger</a></em></p><p>The fallout '
                                      'was immediate and devastating. Scammers '
                                      'used the hijacked X account to launch a '
                                      'fake CLAWD token on Solana. It rocketed '
                                      'to a $16 million market cap within '
                                      'hours but plummeted to near-zero when '
                                      'Steinberger publicly clarified that he '
                                      'had nothing to do with it.</p><p>On '
                                      'Jan. 29, Steinberger <a '
                                      'href="https://openclaw.ai/blog/introducing-openclaw" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">announced</a> that the '
                                      'project would once again change its '
                                      'name, this time to OpenClaw, '
                                      'representing both its open-source '
                                      'nature and lobster '
                                      'heritage.</p><p>There have been other '
                                      'issues as well. Experts quickly '
                                      'identified that many users were running '
                                      'OpenClaw with exposed <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/api" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">API</a> keys and open server '
                                      'ports, effectively leaving the "master '
                                      'keys" to their digital lives reachable '
                                      'by any hacker with an IP '
                                      'scanner.</p><p>Plus, there are mounting '
                                      'reports that Anthropic is actively '
                                      'banning users powering their OpenClaw '
                                      'with their Claude credentials, '
                                      'seemingly for violating a clause in its '
                                      '<a '
                                      'href="https://www.anthropic.com/legal/consumer-terms" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">Terms of Service</a> that '
                                      'says its services cannot be used to '
                                      'develop or train any AI or machine '
                                      'learning algorithms or models.</p><h2 '
                                      'id="crypto-use-cases-for-openclaw">Crypto '
                                      'Use Cases for OpenClaw</h2><p>Despite '
                                      'the user security issues and rough '
                                      'start, the crypto community has '
                                      'embraced OpenClaw for what many say is '
                                      'an unparalleled ability to act as an '
                                      'autonomous <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/on-chain" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">on-chain</a> '
                                      'agent.</p><p>Here are some of the most '
                                      'ambitious use cases users have come up '
                                      'with so far.</p><h3 '
                                      'id="polymarket-yield-sniper">Polymarket '
                                      'Yield Sniper</h3><p>Prediction markets '
                                      'like <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/article/polymarket-lands-major-league-soccer-partnership-deal" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">Polymarket</a> move at the '
                                      'speed of news. Users are now using '
                                      'OpenClaw to monitor global news feeds '
                                      'and social media sentiment in real time '
                                      'to automate their "Yes/No" '
                                      'positions.</p><p>Using this execution '
                                      'loop, Polymarket traders can reduce the '
                                      'lag that usually costs them the best '
                                      'entry. The practical result is less '
                                      '“human delay” and more opportunities to '
                                      'extract gains from mispricings, an '
                                      'advantage normally reserved for teams '
                                      'running institutional-grade '
                                      'bots.</p><img '
                                      'src="https://academy-public.coinmarketcap.com/optimized-uploads/c22afe9fbef8444aa685704ed79668e1.png"><p><em>Source: '
                                      '<a '
                                      'href="https://twitter.com/0xMovez/status/2016285520825835834" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">Movez</a></em></p><h3 '
                                      'id="autonomous-researcher-trader">Autonomous '
                                      'Researcher/Trader</h3><p>Traditional '
                                      'research can be a slog, involving hours '
                                      'of scrolling through white papers and '
                                      'reports, aggregating on-chain data, and '
                                      'writing up briefs. OpenClaw users are '
                                      'now creating "research agents" that '
                                      'aggregate data into an easy-to-read '
                                      'format for quick decision-making. The '
                                      'result gives traders quicker reads on '
                                      'whether a <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/token" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">token</a> is real, liquid, '
                                      'and/or worth deeper '
                                      'attention.</p><p>Others have gone one '
                                      'step further, giving their OpenClaw '
                                      'live access to a funded crypto <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/wallet" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">wallet</a> and directing it '
                                      'to trade automatically using its '
                                      'insights.</p><img '
                                      'src="https://academy-public.coinmarketcap.com/optimized-uploads/eb93cac096e14efd9dfd05cf92f0d3c8.jpg"><p><em>Source: '
                                      '<a '
                                      'href="https://twitter.com/Legendaryy/status/2015101399047274517" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">Legendary</a></em></p><p>That '
                                      'said, it’s important to separate the '
                                      'hype from the reality here. Most '
                                      'positive results are unsubstantiated, '
                                      'but the proof of concept is '
                                      'there.</p><p><strong><em>Don’t have '
                                      'time to get to grips with OpenClaw? <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/cmc-ai/ask/" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow">Give '
                                      'CMC AI a try</a>.</em></strong></p><h3 '
                                      'id="passive-airdrop-farming-at-scale">Passive '
                                      '"Airdrop Farming" at Scale</h3><p><a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/airdrop" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">Airdrop</a> farming can be '
                                      'incredibly tedious and repetitive, '
                                      'especially when taken to '
                                      'extremes.</p><p>Farmers are using '
                                      'OpenClaw to automate "proof of '
                                      'activity" across dozens of testnets, '
                                      'allowing them to automatically perform '
                                      'daily tasks like bridging small amounts '
                                      'of Ethereum (<a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/ethereum/" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">ETH</a>), swapping on a <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/decentralized-exchange-dex" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">decentralized exchange '
                                      '(DEX)</a>, or interacting with Discord '
                                      'channels to maintain "active" '
                                      'status.</p><img '
                                      'src="https://academy-public.coinmarketcap.com/optimized-uploads/8920dcba6b3b4bb3a6a1ac550d72dda8.png"><p><em>Source: '
                                      '<a '
                                      'href="https://twitter.com/YieldPrime/status/2015578297854550382" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">Yield '
                                      'Prime</a></em></p><p>Because OpenClaw '
                                      'runs locally, it can maintain '
                                      'persistent sessions across multiple '
                                      'browser profiles without triggering '
                                      '"bot detection" as easily as '
                                      'cloud-based scripts. Some people are '
                                      'already using this to bypass the '
                                      'typical Sybil defenses used by many '
                                      'airdrop campaigns.</p><p>The result is '
                                      'greater consistency and less manual '
                                      'effort, turning a tedious grind into a '
                                      'systematic workflow — albeit at the '
                                      'expense of projects seeking genuine '
                                      'user engagement.</p><h3 '
                                      'id="on-chain-sentry">On-Chain '
                                      'Sentry</h3><p>Another unique OpenClaw '
                                      'crypto workflow is running it as a 24/7 '
                                      'market sentinel.</p><p>Creative users '
                                      'are utilizing it to track <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/whale" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">whale</a> wallets and monitor '
                                      'big exchange inflows/outflows, '
                                      'liquidation spikes, and open interest '
                                      'changes, as well as sudden narrative '
                                      'shifts. It can then ping them on '
                                      'Telegram/Discord the moment something '
                                      'interesting happens.</p><img '
                                      'src="https://academy-public.coinmarketcap.com/optimized-uploads/e0bbdf80ca62490099707c4faae5094a.jpg"><p><em>Source: '
                                      '<a '
                                      'href="https://x.com/reMugi3/status/2016089315491598713" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">Mugi</a></em></p><p>Instead '
                                      'of manually refreshing dashboards, the '
                                      'bot uses loops to automatically scan, '
                                      'detect, , giving you an edge over those '
                                      'still doing it the “old-fashioned '
                                      'way.”</p><h2 '
                                      'id="crypto-and-the-agentic-economy">Crypto '
                                      'and the Agentic Economy</h2><p>If the '
                                      'agentic economy takes off, what will '
                                      'agents use to transact?</p><p>An AI '
                                      'agent can’t walk into a bank, pass <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/kyc" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow">Know '

                                                          'blockchain and '

                                                          'sectors.',

                                           'id': '63637417847d336a2db377f9',

                           'content': '<p>In the fast-moving world of '
                                      'artificial intelligence, a week is a '
                                      'long time. For OpenClaw (formerly '
                                      'Clawdbot and then MoltBot), the last '
                                      'seven days have been a masterclass in '
                                      'viral growth, but trademark disputes '
                                      'and a stream of mishaps have marred its '
                                      'potential.</p><p>What started as a '
                                      'niche tool for developers has turned '
                                      'into a cautionary tale of "The Great '
                                      'Molt" — a rebrand that saw millions of '
                                      'dollars vanish in minutes while '
                                      'simultaneously cementing OpenClaw as a '
                                      'potential game-changer for crypto '
                                      'degens.</p><h2 '
                                      'id="what-is-openclaw">What is '
                                      'OpenClaw?</h2><p>OpenClaw is a '
                                      'self-hosted <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/ai-agents" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow">AI '
                                      'agent</a> assistant created by '
                                      'developer Peter Steinberger. Unlike a '
                                      'standard chatbot, OpenClaw is designed '
                                      'to live on your local hardware — often '
                                      'a Mac Mini — and execute real-world '
                                      'tasks.</p><p>It can:</p><ul><li>Control '
                                      'your browser</li><li>Run terminal '
                                      'commands</li><li>Plan and execute tasks '
                                      'autonomously</li><li>Communicate with '
                                      'you via WhatsApp or '
                                      'Telegram.</li></ul><p>Its "local-first" '
                                      'architecture means your private data '
                                      'never leaves your server, a feature '
                                      'that has made it popular among '
                                      'privacy-conscious crypto '
                                      'natives.</p><p>It launched publicly in '
                                      'November 2025, but didn’t gain '
                                      'widespread attention until December '
                                      '2025 following the success of '
                                      'Anthropic’s Claude Code.</p><p>OpenClaw '
                                      'has since gone viral, <a '
                                      'href="https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">recording</a> over 100,000 '
                                      'stars on <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/github" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">GitHub</a> (similar to '
                                      'Instagram likes) and securing mentions '
                                      'in high-profile publications including '
                                      '<a '
                                      'href="https://www.forbes.com/sites/ronschmelzer/2026/01/27/viral-ai-sidekick-clawdbot-changes-name-to-moltbot-and-sheds-its-old-skin/" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">Forbes</a> and <a '
                                      'href="https://www.wired.com/story/clawdbot-moltbot-viral-ai-assistant/" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">WIRED</a> — making it one of '
                                      'the most visible <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/open-source" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">open-source</a> agent tools '
                                      'right now.</p><h2 id="the-molt">The '
                                      '“Molt”</h2><p>OpenClaw’s rise to glory '
                                      'wasn’t without hiccups. The project was '
                                      'originally named Clawdbot, but it '
                                      'received a trademark request from AI '
                                      'development firm Anthropic due to its '
                                      'similarity to their "Claude" large '
                                      'language model.</p><p>The team pivoted '
                                      'to the name MoltBot — a metaphor for a '
                                      'lobster shedding its shell to '
                                      'grow.</p><p>But the transition became a '
                                      'disaster when the team attempted to '
                                      'swap their social media '
                                      'handles.</p><p>In the few seconds '
                                      'between dropping the old @clawdbot '
                                      'handle and claiming the new one, '
                                      'professional "handle snipers" snagged '
                                      'it.</p><img '
                                      'src="https://academy-public.coinmarketcap.com/optimized-uploads/1d2f30a6b2f04bd28dc56593bdde5e02.jpg"><p><em>Source: '
                                      '<a '
                                      'href="https://twitter.com/steipete/status/2016068265391354181" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">Peter '
                                      'Steinberger</a></em></p><p>The fallout '
                                      'was immediate and devastating. Scammers '
                                      'used the hijacked X account to launch a '
                                      'fake CLAWD token on Solana. It rocketed '
                                      'to a $16 million market cap within '
                                      'hours but plummeted to near-zero when '
                                      'Steinberger publicly clarified that he '
                                      'had nothing to do with it.</p><p>On '
                                      'Jan. 29, Steinberger <a '
                                      'href="https://openclaw.ai/blog/introducing-openclaw" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">announced</a> that the '
                                      'project would once again change its '
                                      'name, this time to OpenClaw, '
                                      'representing both its open-source '
                                      'nature and lobster '
                                      'heritage.</p><p>There have been other '
                                      'issues as well. Experts quickly '
                                      'identified that many users were running '
                                      'OpenClaw with exposed <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/api" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">API</a> keys and open server '
                                      'ports, effectively leaving the "master '
                                      'keys" to their digital lives reachable '
                                      'by any hacker with an IP '
                                      'scanner.</p><p>Plus, there are mounting '
                                      'reports that Anthropic is actively '
                                      'banning users powering their OpenClaw '
                                      'with their Claude credentials, '
                                      'seemingly for violating a clause in its '
                                      '<a '
                                      'href="https://www.anthropic.com/legal/consumer-terms" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">Terms of Service</a> that '
                                      'says its services cannot be used to '
                                      'develop or train any AI or machine '
                                      'learning algorithms or models.</p><h2 '
                                      'id="crypto-use-cases-for-openclaw">Crypto '
                                      'Use Cases for OpenClaw</h2><p>Despite '
                                      'the user security issues and rough '
                                      'start, the crypto community has '
                                      'embraced OpenClaw for what many say is '
                                      'an unparalleled ability to act as an '
                                      'autonomous <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/on-chain" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">on-chain</a> '
                                      'agent.</p><p>Here are some of the most '
                                      'ambitious use cases users have come up '
                                      'with so far.</p><h3 '
                                      'id="polymarket-yield-sniper">Polymarket '
                                      'Yield Sniper</h3><p>Prediction markets '
                                      'like <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/article/polymarket-lands-major-league-soccer-partnership-deal" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">Polymarket</a> move at the '
                                      'speed of news. Users are now using '
                                      'OpenClaw to monitor global news feeds '
                                      'and social media sentiment in real time '
                                      'to automate their "Yes/No" '
                                      'positions.</p><p>Using this execution '
                                      'loop, Polymarket traders can reduce the '
                                      'lag that usually costs them the best '
                                      'entry. The practical result is less '
                                      '“human delay” and more opportunities to '
                                      'extract gains from mispricings, an '
                                      'advantage normally reserved for teams '
                                      'running institutional-grade '
                                      'bots.</p><img '
                                      'src="https://academy-public.coinmarketcap.com/optimized-uploads/c22afe9fbef8444aa685704ed79668e1.png"><p><em>Source: '
                                      '<a '
                                      'href="https://twitter.com/0xMovez/status/2016285520825835834" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">Movez</a></em></p><h3 '
                                      'id="autonomous-researcher-trader">Autonomous '
                                      'Researcher/Trader</h3><p>Traditional '
                                      'research can be a slog, involving hours '
                                      'of scrolling through white papers and '
                                      'reports, aggregating on-chain data, and '
                                      'writing up briefs. OpenClaw users are '
                                      'now creating "research agents" that '
                                      'aggregate data into an easy-to-read '
                                      'format for quick decision-making. The '
                                      'result gives traders quicker reads on '
                                      'whether a <a '
                                      'href="https://coinmarketcap.com/academy/glossary/token" '
                                      'rel="noopener noreferrer '
                                      'nofollow">token</a> is real, liquid, '
                                      'and/or worth deeper '
                                      'attention.</p><p>Others have gone one '
                                      'step further, giving their OpenClaw '
                                      'live access to a funded crypto <a '

[[reply_to_current]] 这篇讲的是 OpenClaw 最近在币圈/CT 上为什么突然火了，也顺手把它的争议一起写了。核心意思大概是：

- **OpenClaw 是什么**
  - 一个**自托管 AI agent assistant**
  - 不是普通聊天机器人，而是能直接帮你做事的 agent
  - 可以：
    - 控制浏览器
    - 运行终端命令
    - 自动规划和执行任务
    - 接消息、回消息
    - 接各种工具和服务
  - 典型部署方式是跑在你自己的本地设备上，比如 Mac Mini

- **为什么它火**
  - 因为它很贴近大家对“真正 AI 助手”的想象：不是只会聊天，而是能**动手**
  - 尤其在 crypto Twitter 这种场景里，“能自己看网页、发消息、跑任务”的 agent 很容易出圈
  - 文章把它描述成一个从开发者工具迅速变成病毒式传播产品的案例

- **文章里提到的品牌变化**
  - OpenClaw 之前经历过名字变化：
    - **Clawdbot**
    - **MoltBot**
    - **OpenClaw**
  - 里面还提到所谓 **“The Great Molt”**，意思是那次改名和相关风波带来了很大的市场波动和混乱

- **文章的态度**
  - 不是纯吹
  - 它一边说 OpenClaw 很有潜力，可能会成为很强的 agent 平台
  - 一边也强调：
    - 商标/品牌争议
    - 舆论事故
    - 币圈叙事放大后的风险
  - 所以整体更像：**“很有前途，但被一堆混乱事件裹挟了”**

如果你要，我可以继续帮你做两种版本：

1. **中文精简翻译版**
2. **带点评的拆解版**：哪些地方是在说技术，哪些地方是在写币圈 narrative。

content.text:
---
name: web-search-pro
description: |
  Multi-engine web search with full parameter control. Supports Tavily, Exa, Serper, and SerpAPI
  with domain filtering, date ranges, deep search, news mode, and content extraction.
  Auto-selects the best engine based on query type and available API keys.
  多引擎精细化搜索：支持域名过滤、日期范围、深度搜索、新闻模式、内容提取。
  根据查询类型和可用 API Key 自动选择最优引擎。
homepage: https://github.com/Zjianru/web-search-pro
metadata:
  openclaw:
    emoji: "🔎"
    always: true
    requires:
      bins: ["node"]
      env: ["TAVILY_API_KEY"]
    primaryEnv: "TAVILY_API_KEY"
---

# Web Search Pro

Multi-engine web search with full parameter control for AI agents.
A precision supplement to OpenClaw's built-in `web_search` (Brave/Perplexity), providing
domain filtering, deep search, news mode, date ranges, and content extraction
that the built-in search does not support.

多引擎精细化网络搜索，为 AI Agent 设计。
作为 OpenClaw 内置 `web_search`（Brave/Perplexity）的精细化补充，提供域名过滤、
深度搜索、新闻模式、日期范围、内容提取等内置搜索不支持的能力。
配置一个或多个 API Key，自动选择最优引擎。

## Engines / 引擎

| Engine | Strengths | Free Tier | API Key Env |
|--------|-----------|-----------|-------------|
| **Tavily** | AI-optimized, best answer quality, full filters, extract | 1000/month | `TAVILY_API_KEY` |
| **Exa** | Semantic/neural search, deep research | $10 credit | `EXA_API_KEY` |
| **Serper** | Real Google SERP, best news coverage | 100/month | `SERPER_API_KEY` |
| **SerpAPI** | Multi-engine (Google/Bing/Baidu/Yandex/DuckDuckGo) | 250/month | `SERPAPI_API_KEY` |

## Auto-Select Priority / 自动选择优先级

When `--engine` is not specified, the skill picks the best available engine:

| Query Type | Priority | Reason |
|------------|----------|--------|
| Default | Tavily > Exa > Serper > SerpAPI | Tavily has best AI answer + full filters |
| `--deep` | Tavily > Exa | Both have dedicated deep search modes |
| `--news` | Serper > Tavily | Google News has widest coverage |
| `--news --days` | Tavily only | `--days` is Tavily news-specific parameter |
| `--include-domains` | Tavily > Exa > Serper > SerpAPI | Tavily/Exa have native domain filters |
| `--search-engine baidu` | SerpAPI | Only SerpAPI supports Baidu/Yandex |

## Search / 搜索

```bash
# Basic search (auto-select engine)
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query"

# Force specific engine
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --engine tavily

# Domain filtering (only search specific sites)
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --include-domains "github.com,stackoverflow.com"

# Exclude domains
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --exclude-domains "pinterest.com,quora.com"

# Date range (absolute)
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --from 2026-01-01 --to 2026-02-09

# Time range (relative)
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --time-range week

# Deep/advanced search
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --deep

# News search
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --news --days 7

# Multi-engine: Baidu search
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --engine serpapi --search-engine baidu

# More results
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" -n 10

# JSON output (for programmatic use)
node {baseDir}/scripts/search.mjs "query" --json
```

## Extract / 内容提取

Extract readable content from URLs (Tavily Extract or Exa livecrawl):

```bash
node {baseDir}/scripts/extract.mjs "https://example.com/article"
node {baseDir}/scripts/extract.mjs "url1" "url2" "url3"
node {baseDir}/scripts/extract.mjs "url" --engine exa
node {baseDir}/scripts/extract.mjs "url" --json
```

## All Options / 全部参数

| Option | Description | Engines |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `--engine <name>` | Force engine: tavily\|exa\|serper\|serpapi | all |
| `-n <count>` | Number of results (default: 5) | all |
| `--deep` | Deep/advanced search mode | tavily, exa |
| `--news` | News search mode | tavily, serper, serpapi |
| `--days <n>` | Limit news to last N days | tavily |
| `--include-domains <d,...>` | Only search these domains | all (native: tavily, exa) |
| `--exclude-domains <d,...>` | Exclude these domains | all (native: tavily, exa) |
| `--time-range <range>` | day\|week\|month\|year | all |
| `--from <YYYY-MM-DD>` | Results after this date | all |
| `--to <YYYY-MM-DD>` | Results before this date | all |
| `--search-engine <name>` | SerpAPI sub-engine: google\|bing\|baidu\|yandex\|duckduckgo | serpapi |
| `--country <code>` | Country code (us, cn, de...) | serper, serpapi |
| `--lang <code>` | Language code (en, zh, de...) | serper, serpapi |
| `--json` | Raw JSON output | all |

## Setup / 配置

Add API keys to your environment (e.g., `~/.openclaw/.env`):

```bash
# Configure at least one (recommended: Tavily)
TAVILY_API_KEY=tvly-xxxxx        # https://tavily.com (1000 free/month)
EXA_API_KEY=exa-xxxxx            # https://exa.ai ($10 free credit)
SERPER_API_KEY=xxxxx             # https://serper.dev (100 free/month)
SERPAPI_API_KEY=xxxxx            # https://serpapi.com (250 free/month)
```

## Notes / 说明

- At least one API key must be configured
- Metadata declares runtime prerequisites explicitly:
  - `requires.bins`: `node`
  - `requires.env`: `TAVILY_API_KEY` (primary onboarding key)
  - `primaryEnv`: `TAVILY_API_KEY`
- Other provider keys (`EXA_API_KEY`, `SERPER_API_KEY`, `SERPAPI_API_KEY`) are optional and used when `--engine`/auto-select routes to those engines
- `--search-engine <name>` always uses SerpAPI and requires `SERPAPI_API_KEY`
- `--deep` only works on Tavily/Exa; if both keys are missing, command exits with an error
- `--news` only works on Tavily/Serper/SerpAPI; if none are available, command exits with an error
- `--days` only works with Tavily news mode
- `--news --days <n>` auto-selects Tavily; if Tavily key is missing, command exits with an error
- Domain filtering via `--include-domains`/`--exclude-domains` works natively on Tavily and Exa; on Serper/SerpAPI it's implemented via `site:` query operators
- `--deep` mode uses more API credits (Tavily: 2x, Exa: varies)
- Extract only works with Tavily and Exa
- Missing/invalid option values fail fast with explicit CLI error messages (no silent fallback, no runtime TypeError)
- All output is Markdown-formatted for AI agent consumption; use `--json` for programmatic access
